title: Harden the shared write-guard matcher to cover Codex apply_patch; regenerate all projections
action: Harden the write-guard matcher so it explicitly covers Codex's apply_patch tool, and correct the over-tight byte-identity framing that failed 6c's validation (Gate-2 approved 2026-07-12; plan-repo-fallback-split.md). Evidence (validation-codex-parity-6c.md): Codex matches the matcher against the alias set {apply_patch, Edit, Write}, so bee's Edit|Write already fires on apply_patch — but that silently depends on Codex keeping the alias and gives zero coverage on Codex older than PR #18391. (1) In hooks/catalog.mjs add `apply_patch` to the shared runtimes:BOTH write-guard matcher (line ~121), e.g. `apply_patch|Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash|Read|Glob|Grep`. This is one shared string that renders into all three projections; Claude Code exposes no apply_patch tool so the extra alternative is an inert no-op in the Claude projection and load-bearing in the Codex projection. NO per-runtime divergence and NO new ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES entry is required. (2) Regenerate ALL THREE projections from the catalog: hooks/hooks.json, hooks/claude-hooks.json, and .codex/hooks.json — their matcher-line bytes change, which is CORRECT: real D1 (CONTEXT.md:25) is plugin-first distribution / exactly one active source, NOT byte-identity. The catalog-drift rows (codex-projection-byte-identical / claude-projection-byte-identical) compare render-vs-disk and will pass once regenerated. (3) In hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs add a matcher-assertion route row: the harness today executes command strings but never evaluates the matcher — assert that the PreToolUse write-guard matcher matches `apply_patch` (and still matches Edit/Write/Bash). (4) Do NOT keep any exact-match `git diff --exit-code` pin against 7499a71 for hooks.json/claude-hooks.json — that is what made 6c's verify unsatisfiable; the matcher-tolerant pin in this cell's verify asserts the ONLY changed lines are matcher lines. NOTE the stated dependency: the live Codex apply_patch deny is a post-merge Gate-4 UAT (interactive trusted session, apply_patch to .bee/state.json expects the guard exit 2; a non-gated .spikes/ target expects exit 0). If that UAT unexpectedly shows the matcher does NOT route apply_patch on Codex, a follow-on cell makes apply_patch a codex-only ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES matcher — do not pre-build that here.

truths: the shared runtimes:BOTH write-guard matcher string includes apply_patch
hooks/hooks.json, hooks/claude-hooks.json and .codex/hooks.json are all regenerated from the catalog and their only change versus 7499a71 is the matcher line gaining apply_patch
the catalog-drift byte-identity rows pass because the projections were regenerated (render == disk), not hand-edited
a test row evaluates the actual PreToolUse write-guard matcher and asserts it matches apply_patch as well as Edit/Write/Bash
no per-runtime matcher divergence and no new ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES entry were introduced

artifacts.substantive: shared write-guard matcher includes apply_patch; single source rendering into all three projections
regenerated; matcher line gains apply_patch (behaviorally inert on Claude, byte-changed)
regenerated; matcher now explicitly names apply_patch (load-bearing on Codex)
a matcher-assertion row proves the write-guard matcher routes apply_patch

key_links: apply_patch in the shared matcher removes the silent dependency on Codex's Edit/Write alias and covers pre-#18391 Codex
regenerating the projections is compatible with D1 because D1 governs distribution, not byte-identity
the matcher-assertion row is the first test that evaluates the matcher rather than only the command string
prohibitions: do not touch the repo transport / marker walk / --repo-root — that is codex-parity-6c
do not add a codex-only ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES matcher unless the post-merge UAT proves the alias does not route
do not hand-edit the projection JSON — regenerate from the catalog
must_haves.red_failure_evidence: Before edits, show new RegExp('Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash|Read|Glob|Grep').test('apply_patch') === false — the current matcher does not name apply_patch; capture that plus the current identical matcher line in hooks/hooks.json:28, hooks/claude-hooks.json:28, .codex/hooks.json:28.

verify: node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --catalog-only && ! node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && [ -z "$(git diff 7499a71 -- hooks/hooks.json hooks/claude-hooks.json | grep -E '^[-+]' | grep -vE '^(\+\+\+|---)' | grep -v '"matcher":')" ] && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs

trace.dropped_reason: Dropped with 6c: the apply_patch matcher hardening rode on the 6c slice which the human stopped. The matcher already fires on Codex via the {apply_patch,Edit,Write} alias (validation-codex-parity-6c.md); explicit hardening is a nice-to-have, refiled as backlog if wanted, not built now.
